answer:
I remember a time when I helped a classmate who was really struggling with math. We had this major exam coming up, and she was feeling super overwhelmed. I mean, math has never been my favorite subject either, but I thought, why not lend a hand? So, I invited her over to my place one weekend to study together. 

At first, it was a little awkward, you know? But as we went through the problems step by step, she started to grasp the concepts better. I could see her confidence grow, and honestly, that was really rewarding for me. There was this moment when she finally solved a tough equation all by herself, and I could tell how excited she was. It felt amazing to know that I played a part in helping her feel that way.

After our study session, she told me that she appreciated my help so much and that it made a huge difference for her. I think it's interesting how sometimes, just being there for someone can create such a positive impact without expecting anything in return. I didn't want any recognition; seeing her succeed was enough for me. 

Honestly, it kind of reminded me that we all face challenges, and helping each other out, even in small ways, can really lift us up. It's moments like these that show the importance of kindness and support in our lives. And, well, it made me realize that collaboration can lead to incredible outcomes, not just academically, but personally too.
title: Tell me about a time you helped someone without wanting anything back. Who did you help? How did it make them feel?
